meta.purpose: Machine-readable index of every type across the four domain schemas plus the architecture draft and reference library. Canonical home, stereotype, cross-references, and conflict flags. Analysis only — no schema edits applied.

drawing.noteOnCount: Prior session described this as 22 types; actual MODEL count is 23 (A5+B4+C6+D2+E4+F2).

architecture.note: Prose+diagram artifact; no machine-readable MODEL object. Seed for the missing compute-locality spec.

stereotypeVocabulary.interface: design-standard only — reuses the render color slot for the «interface/seam» stereotype; a 6th stereotype not present in the original five.

meta.baseUnitInvariant: Linear geometry stored as integer ticks @ 1/32in. Area/volume/weight/price derived as real. 1in = 32 ticks.

LumberStock.notes: design-standard renames LumberStock -> Stock; same entity, two names. Reconcile to one.

referencedBy: solver.cut.Piece (re-carded as solver output, correctly cross-referenced)
Piece.notes: Canonical home asserted in materials card text. Solver re-cards it but explicitly defers definition to materials. No true duplication.

Dimensions.notes: Reference example for the tick invariant: length/actualWidth/actualThickness are int ticks; area/volume derived real.

MechanicalProperties.notes: Wood design values (Fb,Ft,Fc,FcPerp,Fv,E,Emin) modeled in THREE schemas. S1 finding. Plus adjustmentFactors map mixes intrinsic + contextual factors (split violation).

Weight.notes: Only existing piece of the loads layer (self-weight mass). Should feed the proposed loads-analysis dead-load rollup.

duplicatedBy: solver.StockOption.price (as bare 'cents' int, not a PriceQuote ref)
PriceQuote.notes: Money is float USD here, integer cents in solver. Pick one representation; solver should reference PriceQuote, not inline cents.

Transform.notes: Pure geometry primitive (origin+rotation). Used by 3 schemas. Should live in shared geometry-kernel, not a single-member domain schema.

BoundingBox.notes: Pure AABB geometry primitive. Same misplacement as Transform.

ConnectionPoint.notes: Per-member fastener location/normal. The aggregate ConnectionGraph (design-standard) that consumes these has no built home in materials/solver yet.

referencedBy: drawing(ToolDefinition note cites it as the flyweight exemplar)
LumberSpec.notes: Exemplary flyweight. Cited by every other schema as the pattern reference.

SupplierSku.notes: Flyweight. Solver re-cards it (correctly cross-referenced). Minor field drift: solver lists price as cents; materials uses PriceQuote.

CutOperation.notes: Consumes the shared Plane primitive (CutOperation.plane). design-standard refers to it as 'Cut'.

RenderMesh.notes: Render adapter inside a domain schema. The schema itself states 'render lives outside the domain' — yet RenderMesh/MeshInstance live in it. Extract to a render artifact.

MeshInstance.notes: Correct intrinsic/contextual flyweight split (RenderMesh intrinsic geometry + per-instance Transform). But the whole render layer belongs out of materials.

Volume.notes: Lives in solver's Geometry Kernel layer but drawing+architecture treat the kernel (truck/BREP) as a separate module. Decide: own geometry-kernel artifact vs solver layer.

Face.notes: Face.plane consumes the shared Plane primitive. Face.area typed 'ticks²' — base-unit deviation (area should be real in²).

PushPullOp.notes: Workspace emits, kernel consumes. Same home ambiguity as Volume/Face.

Wall.notes: Stud count derived, never stored — correct.

Junction.notes: Owner-wall pattern resolves double-counting. Sound DDD.

MemberPlacement.notes: Exemplary intrinsic/contextual split: shared spec + per-instance install context (orientation, bracing, ends).

NDSDesignValues.notes: Flyweight correctly applied, but the type itself duplicates MechanicalProperties. Card note admits 'lives near MechanicalProperties in the materials schema.' Reconcile to one wood design-value flyweight.

AdjustmentFactors.notes: NDS factor stack modeled 3-4 times. Solver correctly treats it as contextual (role selects the stack); materials wrongly folds it into the intrinsic flyweight.

LoadCase.notes: Loads modeled inside the structural layer here, in the core in design-standard. Should move to a dedicated loads-analysis artifact.

CapacityCheck.notes: Solver's flat NDS-specific check is re-modeled by design-standard's Strategy seam. Two competing architectures for the same pipeline stage. Reconcile: solver structural layer becomes the NDS leaf behind the seam.

StockOption.notes: price typed 'cents' — drift vs materials PriceQuote (float USD).

Camera.notes: Camera eye/target/up legitimately float (view math, not committed geometry).

CoordinateFrame.notes: Global origin + X/Y/Z basis. Pure shared geometry primitive; belongs in geometry-kernel alongside Plane/Transform.

Plane.notes: Known collision #1. Cut plane = face plane = sketch plane (origin+basis+normal). Defined in drawing but consumed by materials+solver. Extract to geometry-kernel; all three reference it.

SketchPlane.notes: Correctly wraps a Plane + U/V. Stays in drawing; the inner Plane moves down.

SnapTarget.notes: point typed 'vec3 (resolved to tick)' — committed world point should be vec3<int> ticks, not bare vec3. Soft base-unit deviation.

MeasurementGrammar.notes: Flyweight, looked up by key. Consistent with LumberSpec/ToolDefinition pattern.

ToolDefinition.notes: Flyweight catalog, explicitly modeled on LumberSpec/SupplierSku. Exemplary DRY.

DrawOp.notes: payload 'in world ticks' stated in prose but not typed. The workspace->kernel boundary contract.

ConstructionGeometry.notes: Deliberately non-promotable so guides never reach the solver. Good boundary discipline.

SnapProvenance.notes: Seed of associativity. Distinct from materials.ProvenanceLink and design-standard.Provenance — three 'provenance' concepts, worth a shared vocabulary check.

Stock.notes: Material-agnostic rename of LumberStock + material/stockForm discriminators. Same entity; reconcile names across schemas.

SectionProperties.notes: Pure geometry (A,S,I). Real units in²/in³/in⁴ — consistent with materials, inconsistent with solver Face.area ticks².

LoadCombination.notes: ASCE 7 combos. Should be single-homed in the loads-analysis artifact.

BeamStatics.notes: M=wL²/8 etc. Statics-only, E injected by strategy. No analog in solver — solver lacks an explicit statics service.

SizingArbiter.notes: Carries the ASD/LRFD philosophy; orchestrates the seam. The reconciled home for solver's CapacityCheck.

LimitStateCheck.notes: Uniform demand/capacity record; origin=core|strategy. Cleaner than solver's flat 'mode' enum.

ConnectionGraph.notes: First-class connection graph (nodes=Piece[], edges=Connection[]). The aggregate that materials.ConnectionPoint feeds. No built home before this; reconcile ownership.

Provenance.notes: Three provenance concepts across schemas. Not identical, but the vocabulary should be deliberately unified.

PrescriptiveLookup.notes: Material-blind table lookup; reuses reference-library subjectIndex shape.

referencedBy: all Tier-1 core types call through it
DesignStandard.notes: The Strategy seam. Implementations: NDS_Wood, AISI_CFS, AISC, ACI_Concrete, TMS. 6th stereotype («interface») not in the original five.

NDS_Wood.notes: The wood leaf. Its designValues+factors are the THIRD copy of the wood design-value model. Should consume the single MechanicalProperties flyweight.

ACI_Concrete.notes: Concrete leaf, stubbed. Routes to formwork/volume output, bypassing the linear cut optimizer (gotcha #3).

collisions.summary: Wood structural design values (Fb,Ft,Fc,FcPerp,Fv,E,Emin) modeled in three schemas.
collisions.resolution: Single wood design-value flyweight in materials (MechanicalProperties); solver+design-standard reference it. NDS_Wood becomes the leaf that points at it.
One AdjustmentFactors value object; base reference values intrinsic (flyweight), the applied stack contextual (per MemberPlacement). Remove the factors map from materials.MechanicalProperties.

collisions.summary: The structural-check pipeline stage has two competing schemas (solver flat NDS vs design-standard Strategy seam).
collisions.resolution: design-standard's seam subsumes solver's structural layer; solver's NDS types become the NDS_Wood leaf behind the seam.

collisions.summary: Known collision #1: Plane defined in drawing, consumed by materials + solver.
collisions.resolution: Extract Plane to geometry-kernel shared layer.

collisions.summary: Known collision #2: render adapter lives inside the materials domain schema, contradicting the schema's own 'render lives outside the domain' rule. Render also appears in solver(none explicit)/drawing(Camera/Viewport are view, not mesh-render)/architecture(render mirror).
collisions.resolution: Extract RenderMesh/MeshInstance/MaterialRef to a dedicated render-adapter artifact = the JS render-mirror in the system-architecture spec.

collisions.summary: Pure geometry primitives (Transform, BoundingBox, CoordinateFrame) defined inside domain schemas but consumed broadly.
collisions.resolution: Move to geometry-kernel shared-primitives layer alongside Plane.

collisions.summary: Same entity under two names across schemas (rename proposed but not applied).
collisions.resolution: Apply the Stock/StockSpec rename + material discriminator everywhere, or explicitly defer with a documented alias.

collisions.summary: Loads modeled inside structural (solver) and inside core (design-standard); LoadCombination duplicated.
collisions.resolution: Single loads-analysis artifact owns all load types and LoadCombination.
Pick one; solver should reference PriceQuote.

collisions.summary: Three provenance concepts; no shared vocabulary.
collisions.resolution: Define a shared provenance vocabulary; keep distinct types but consistent naming/shape.

collisions.summary: Geometry-kernel types live in solver's layer 1 but drawing+architecture treat the kernel (truck/BREP) as a separate module.
collisions.resolution: Promote to an explicit geometry-kernel artifact (the natural home for Plane/Transform/Path2D/Segment too).

danglingRefs.fix: Should be a ref to RenderMesh.geometryId (render layer).
Define, or unify with the shared provenance vocabulary.
Inline shape {minQty,pricePer} given; promote to a named value object.
Define as the strategy output type; for wood = MechanicalProperties.
Define the edge type (joint + method + capacity).
Document as opaque key string aliases; CitationKey points into reference-library.

sharedPrimitives.proposedHome: geometry-kernel (new artifact or explicit shared layer)

primitives.name: TickVec3 (vec3<int>)
primitives.currentlyTyped: vec3<int> in materials; bare vec3 in solver/drawing

baseUnitAudit.rule: linear geometry = integer ticks @ 1/32in; area/volume/weight/price = real

materials.notes: All linear fields int ticks; area/volume/weight/price float; vec3<int> used for tick positions; normals plain vec3; area explicitly NOT stored as tick². Exemplary.

violations.shouldBe: consistent with materials PriceQuote (float USD) — representation drift

violations.field: positional vec3 fields (Volume.extrudeAxis ok as dir; Wall.baseline Segment, Face boundary)
violations.shouldBe: tick-typed where they carry world position
vec3<int> ticks for committed world points

violations.issue: no units/base-unit declaration; relies on carried-over typed primitives

designStandard.notes: SectionProperties uses real in²/in³/in⁴ (correct, and a counter-example to solver's ticks²). Analysis layer carries little raw linear geometry.

materials.note: No pipeline strip; upstream data model.

drift: loads folded inside Structural Check (LoadCase) not a stage

drawing.coversCanonical: expansion of the 'drawing' stage + handoff to kernel->solver
drift: coherent: this is the drawing sub-pipeline; 'drawings' OUTPUT correctly deferred to a separate downstream schema
folds 'drawing' + 'geometry kernel' into one 'Geometry edit / JS' stage — blurs the JS-edit vs Rust-kernel boundary
'Loads & analysis' present in the Rust machine list but ABSENT from the pipeline strip

driftSummary: Stage label drift: 'building model' (solver, arch) vs 'framing model' (design-standard, canonical); 'structural check' (solver, arch) vs 'structural sizing' (design-standard); 'supplier constraint' vs 'supplier'.
Geometry-kernel stage conflated with JS geometry-edit in architecture.
Loads/analysis is never a pipeline stage in ANY strip (sub-component in solver/design-standard, machine-list item in architecture). Confirms Missing Layer (a).
Drawings-export endpoint: terminal in design-standard+architecture, deferred-as-separate-schema in drawing, absent in solver.

currentFragments: architecture 'Loads & analysis' (Rust machine list item, no schema)

computeLocality.status: PARTIALLY EXISTS as architecture.html — needs MODEL formalization
covers: running totals (implied by incremental dirty propagation)
computeLocality.gap: architecture.html is prose+diagram with NO machine-readable MODEL object and no formal buffer-layout / command-protocol type registry.
computeLocality.proposedArtifact: system-architecture-schema.html (formalize architecture.html into a MODEL object)
computeLocality.rationale: Compute-locality is orthogonal to the domain (HOW it runs, not WHAT it models). Keep it its own artifact — cramming SoA/worker-boundary into a domain schema repeats the very mistake the render-in-materials misplacement makes.

drawingsExport.status: THIRD already-acknowledged gap (user asked for two; flagging this)

flyweightUniformity.verdict: Pattern applied uniformly WITHIN schemas, but the same flyweight is duplicated ACROSS schemas.
flyweightUniformity.crossSchemaViolation: Wood design-value flyweight exists 3x (MechanicalProperties / NDSDesignValues / NDS_Wood). The flyweight is correct each time; the TYPE is not single-sourced.

violations.issue: Folds context-dependent factors (CD load-duration, Cr repetitive, Cp/CL buckling — all placement/use dependent) into the shared/intrinsic flyweight. Solver correctly derives the stack from placement context instead.
violations.fix: Keep base reference values intrinsic; move the applied factor stack to per-placement (contextual).

violations.issue: Duplicates pricing inline instead of referencing the materials.PriceQuote value object.

stereotypeConsistency.issue: design-standard introduces a 6th «interface» stereotype (DesignStandard) reusing the render color slot; the «render» stereotype is present in materials+drawing but absent in solver.
stereotypeConsistency.fix: Either formally adopt «interface» as a 6th stereotype in the shared legend, or recolor; document render's absence in solver as intentional (solver has no presentation types).

S1: Quadruplicated adjustment-factor stack + intrinsic/contextual split violation in materials.MechanicalProperties.adjustmentFactors
Competing structural-check architectures (solver flat vs design-standard Strategy seam)
Plane shared primitive misplaced in drawing (consumed by materials+solver)
S2: Render layer misplaced inside materials domain schema
Geometry primitives (Transform/BoundingBox/CoordinateFrame) misplaced in domain schemas
Loads/analysis has no consistent home + missing pipeline stage
Base-unit: solver Face.area & LoadCase.tributaryArea typed ticks² (should be real in²)
S3: Dangling type refs (MeshRef, ProvenanceLink, PriceTier, MaterialRef, Path2D, Segment, DesignValues, Factor, Connection, PrescriptiveTable, SizingQuery, Result)
Price representation drift (float USD vs int cents)
Stereotype vocabulary (6th interface stereotype; render absent in solver)
